title: assembly
description:
Articulated-asset intermediate representation. Consumed by blender_assemble.py and blender_export_usd.py.

Each part stores world-space metric values. blender_assemble.py back-computes the Blender-native node_scale and node_origin at render time using component_dims.json:
  parent_world_scale   = world_size[parent] / raw_size[parent]   (accumulated; [1,1,1] for root)
  node_scale[axis]     = world_size[axis] / (parent_world_scale[axis] × raw_size[axis])
  node_origin[axis]    = world_center[axis] / parent_world_scale[axis]
                         − (R(euler_deg) × (node_scale × raw_center))[axis]

Dividing by parent_world_scale compensates for Blender scale inheritance (children parented with matrix_parent_inverse=identity inherit the parent node_scale). Children must appear after their parent in the parts array.

root.description: Base directory for resolving relative mesh paths (the run directory).

robot_name.description: Name of the assembled object; used as the URDF <robot name>.

parent.description: Name of the parent part; null for the root. Children must appear after their parent in the parts array.

visual_mesh.description: GLB path (relative to root or absolute) used for high-quality Blender renders.

collision_mesh.description: Collision mesh path (relative to root or absolute). Use the same GLB as visual_mesh: component_glbs/<name>.glb.

world_size.description: World bounding-box dimensions [W, D, H] in metres. blender_assemble.py derives node_scale = world_size / (parent_world_scale × raw_size) from component_dims.json.

world_center.description: World-space centre [x, y, z] in metres of the mesh at its current pose (open for revolute/prismatic joints, closed otherwise). blender_assemble.py derives node_origin = world_center / parent_world_scale − R(euler_deg) × (node_scale × raw_center).

euler_deg.description: XYZ Euler rotation in degrees [roll, pitch, yaw] applied at the part's Blender node. For revolute doors: euler_deg[2]<0 opens a left-hinged door; euler_deg[2]>0 opens a right-hinged door. euler_deg[0]<0 opens a bottom-hinged lid; euler_deg[0]>0 opens a top-hinged lid.
